item.title: From teen hacker to Iron Dome researcher, this founder raised $28M to fight AI phishing
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Ocean, an agentic email security platform, raised funding from Lightspeed Venture Partners.

item.title: Forget the feed: Status AI raises $17M to turn social media into interactive entertainment
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: “Status is built on the premise that the next generation doesn’t want to watch stories,” CEO and co-founder Fai Nur said. “They want to engage with the stories and even live inside them.”

item.title: ‘Survivor’ stars Kyle Fraser and Kamilla Karthigesu introduce a goal-tracking app, Paprclip
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: A new app from Survivor 48 winner Kyle Fraser and castmate Kamilla Karthigesu aims to turn goal tracking into a social experience, pairing users for accountability, daily challenges, and shared progress clips.

item.title: Stilta raises $10.5M from a16z and YC to help companies rediscover the patents they forgot they had
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Stilta announced Tuesday a $10 million seed round led by Andreessen Horowitz. Other investors in the round include YC and operators from companies like OpenAI, Legora, and Lovable.

item.title: South Korea’s LetinAR is building optics behind AI glasses
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: A lens the size of a thumbnail — and the South Korean startup that makes it — could become the optical backbone of the AI glasses era.

item.title: The haves and have-nots of the AI gold rush
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: The vibes around the current AI boom aren't great, even in the tech industry.

item.title: Marketing operating system Nectar Social raises $30M Series A led by Menlo
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: AI-powered marketing platform Nectar Social announced Thursday that it raised a $30 million Series A round led by Menlo Ventures and its Anthology Fund, which was created alongside Anthropic.

item.title: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman takes charge of product strategy
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: OpenAI's latest shake-up comes as the company reportedly plans to combine ChatGPT and its programming product Codex.

item.title: $60B AI chip darling Cerebras almost died early on, burning $8M a month
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Cerebras Systems was 2026's biggest tech IPO so far. But years ago, it burned through hundreds of millions working on a chip many believed impossible.

item.title: The OpenAI trial wraps up, and the Musk founder machine keeps spinning
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec, Sean O'Kane, Anthony Ha, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial came to a close this week, and the final arguments kept circling back to one question: can we trust the people in charge of AI? All of this is playing out as SpaceX charges toward what could be one of the largest IPOs in American history, with a whole generation of founders already spinning out […]

item.title: Meridian Ventures launched a $35M fund with a focus on MBA-deferred founders
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: This new fund will back founders building enterprise technology in the United States. Meridian is agnostic, co-founder Devon Gethers said, noting that the firm has already invested in companies in fintech, logistics, healthcare, and of course, AI.

item.title: Lovable just backed a company that’s looking to bring vibe coding to hardware
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Hardware company Atech raised $800,000 in pre-seed funding, including from a16z’s scout fund, Sequoia Scout Fund, and Nordic Makers.

item.title: Two weeks left: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K equity-free funding is running out. Deadline to apply is May 27. Apply now.

item.title: Clio’s $500M milestone arrives just as Anthropic ups the ante
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Legal tech startups, including Clio, which just hit $500 million in ARR, are seeing massive customer adoption.

item.title: Anduril raises $5B, doubles valuation to $61B
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: After achieving $2.2 billion in revenue in 2025, the defense tech startup has raised another massive round, led by Thrive and a16z, it says.

item.title: Introducing the 6 stages at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 — built for today’s tougher startup market
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: From October 13-15, TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 will feature 200+ sessions across six stages, led by 250+ tech leaders shaping the industry today. Register now to save up to $410, plus 50% off a second pass.

item.title: Kevin Hartz’s A* just closed its third fund with $450M
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: The firm takes a generalist approach, backing companies across categories such as AI applications, fintech, healthcare, and security. The average check size for this fund will be between $3 million and $5 million, with the aim to back at least 30 startups.

item.title: Riding an AI rally, Robinhood preps second retail venture IPO
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Robinhood files confidentially for its second venture fund, this time targeting growth and early-stage startups.

item.title: Korea’s biggest manufacturers back Config, the TSMC of robot data
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: Instead of building robots themselves, the team is focused on a simpler goal — providing data that robots need to learn and operate. They believe that better data will be key to making robots more useful.

item.title: Get ready for the whisper-filled office of the future
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: How will work setups change if we spend more and more time talking to our computers?
